basic_details.job_title: TNPSC CTS (Non - Interview Posts) Recruitment 2026 Notification Out - Apply Online for 461 Posts
basic_details.organization_name: Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission (TNPSC), Chennai
basic_details.short_information: TNPSC is recruiting 461 posts across 45 technical positions in various Government Departments, PSUs and Corporations of Tamil Nadu with Degree/PG Degree/Diploma qualification, last date 25 June 2026.

important_dates.exam_date: Will be announced later on www.tnpsc.gov.in
important_dates.admit_card_date: Available on www.tnpscexams.in (will not be sent by post)

application_fee.general_fee: Exemption available subject to eligibility and free chance limits
application_fee.details: The examination fee should be paid at the time of submitting the online application through Net Banking / Credit card / Debit card / UPI on or before the last date. Service charges as applicable will also be payable. Offline mode of payment (Demand Draft, Postal Order, etc.) will not be accepted. Fee once paid is non-refundable. | Note: Candidates belonging to special categories can avail fee exemption as per eligibility and number of free chances available (details in Annexure II of notification). Fee concession option once chosen cannot be modified after successful submission of online application. Making a false claim for fee exemption will result in rejection of candidature and debarment for one year.

age_limit.maximum_age: 55 years (most posts); No Maximum Age Limit for many posts
age_limit.details: Age limit is as on 01-07-2026. All candidates should have completed 21 years of age for all posts. Maximum age limit varies by post. No maximum age limit means the candidate should not have completed 60 years of age as on 01-07-2026 or at the time of selection/appointment. Date of birth will be verified against SSLC or HSC mark sheet issued by Tamil Nadu Board. | Note: For Agriculture Officer (Extension) (1678): Others – max 32 years (34 years for PG/Ph.D in Agriculture); PwBD – 42 years (44 years for PG/Ph.D). For Horticultural Officer (3001): Others – max 32 years (34 years for PG/Ph.D in Horticulture). The age concession for Ex-Servicemen will not apply to Ex-Servicemen candidates already recruited to any class or service or category as per Tamil Nadu Government Servants (Conditions of Service) Act, 2016. Candidates not belonging to reserved categories who have put in 5 years or more of service since first appointment to Government of India or any State/UT Government are not eligible to apply even if within the age limit.

qualification.details: Candidates must possess the educational and technical qualification prescribed for the post on the date of this Notification (20.05.2026). The UG/PG Degree must have been obtained in the order of studies: SSLC + HSC/Diploma → UG Degree → PG Degree → Ph.D. Candidates must also possess adequate knowledge in Tamil as on the date of this Notification. | Tamil Knowledge Requirement: Candidates must possess adequate knowledge in Tamil as on the date of notification. This is deemed satisfied if Tamil was one of the languages studied at SSLC/HSC/Degree level, or if studied in Tamil medium, or if passed the Second Class Language Test (Full Test) in Tamil conducted by TNPSC. This requirement is not applicable to post codes 1678, 3111, 1842, 3106, and 1843. Candidates who fail to produce Tamil language proof must pass the Second Class Language Test (Full Test) conducted by TNPSC within two years of appointment, failing which they will be discharged from service.

salary_details.pay_scale: Level 8 to Level 20 (as per post; some posts under EPF, GFTR, PFTR, SPF cum GSTR)

The Combined Technical Services Examination (Non-Interview Posts) will be conducted as a single-stage Written Examination. Appearance in both Paper I and Paper II is compulsory; candidates who fail to appear for either paper will not be considered for selection even if they secure minimum qualifying marks. Final selection will be based on total marks obtained in Part B and Part C of Paper I and Paper II
subject to the rule of reservation of appointments.
Stage 1 - Written Examination
(A) For all posts except Translator:
(B) For Translator Post (Post Code 3106):

Paper II and Part B & C of Paper I will be evaluated only if the candidate secures minimum qualifying marks of 40% (60 marks) in Part A of Paper I (Tamil Eligibility Test). Differently abled candidates can avail exemption from writing Part A. Questions in Part B and C of Paper I will be set both in English and Tamil. Paper II will be in English language (Tamil and English for select subject papers as specified in the notification).
Stage 2 - Onscreen Certificate Verification
Based on minimum qualifying marks and rule of reservation
candidates will be called for onscreen certificate verification in the ratio of 1:3 (General category) and 1:2 (all reserved categories).
Stage 3 - Physical Certificate Verification and Counselling
After onscreen certificate verification

candidates will be called for physical certificate verification and counselling (wherever applicable) in the ratio of 1:3 (General) and 1:1.5 (Reserved) for posts with counselling; 1:1.2 (all categories) for posts without counselling. For single-vacancy posts where reservation does not apply
3 candidates will be called.
Ranking / Tie-breaking: If two candidates secure equal marks
the one with higher qualification is ranked above. If qualification is also equal
the senior in age is ranked higher. If age is also the same
the candidate who submitted the application earlier (by application number) is ranked higher.
Physical Fitness Standard for Assistant Superintendent of Approved Schools (Post Code 2078):

physical_eligibility.details: The competition level for TNPSC CTS (Non-Interview Posts) 2026 is expected to vary significantly by post. High-vacancy posts like Assistant Engineer (Agricultural Engineering) (64 posts), Block Health Statistician (62 posts), Assistant Engineer – Rural Development and Panchayat Raj (50 posts), and Assistant Engineer – Highways (38 posts) will attract the most applicants. The single-stage written examination format - without an interview - means the final selection is entirely merit-based on the written test score, placing a premium on subject knowledge, General Studies, and Aptitude preparation. | Low-vacancy posts such as Assistant Engineer (Civil) under Tamil Nadu Urban Habitat Development Board (1 post), Assistant Engineer under Tamil Nadu Pollution Control Board (1 post), System Manager (1 post), and Librarian (1 post) will be highly competitive. Candidates must carefully choose subject papers aligned with their qualifications, as the Paper II syllabus is strictly discipline-specific. The exemption from Tamil Eligibility Test (Part A) for PwBD candidates and the provision for Tamil knowledge through language test within 2 years of joining are noteworthy flexibilities.

how_to_apply: Candidates shall apply online only through www.tnpscexams.in. Candidates must first register under One Time Registration (OTR) on the Commission's website and then proceed to fill the online application separately for this examination. The application link will be live from 27-05-2026. Candidates are advised to apply well before the closing date and not wait till the last minute.
Documents Required to be Uploaded at the time of Online Application:

Recent passport-size colour photograph against white background (taken on or after 20-05-2026; name and date of photography printed at bottom; height 4.5 cm / 170 pixels, width 3.5 cm / 130 pixels; 20 KB–50 KB; JPG; 200 DPI) - taken in a photo studio (selfies/cellphone photos not accepted)
Signature (signed within a box of 6.0 x 2.0 cm using blue/black ink pen on white paper; 10 KB–20 KB; JPG; 200 DPI)
SSLC / HSC / Diploma / Degree / PG Degree Certificate or Provisional Degree Certificate / Consolidated Mark Sheet as proof of educational qualification
Proof of Tamil language knowledge (SSLC/HSC/Degree marksheet showing Tamil studied, or Second Class Language Test certificate from TNPSC)
Age Concession supporting documents (caste/disability/discharge certificate etc., as applicable)
Eye Fitness Certificate from Ophthalmologist in a Government Hospital (mandatory for Post Codes 1767, 3268, 3810, 3811, 3812, 1678, 1972, 1861, 3001, 1860, 1750)
Physical Standards Certificate from Government Medical Officer (mandatory for Post Code 2078 – Assistant Superintendent of Approved Schools)
Disability Certificate in format prescribed in Annexure II (for differently abled candidates claiming Part A exemption)
For equivalence of qualification: copy of the relevant Government Order

For queries: Call Toll-Free No. 18004190958 (all working days, 10:00 AM to 5:45 PM). For OTR/online application queries email [email protected]. Other communications: [email protected] or Secretary, TNPSC, TNPSC Road, V.O.C. Nagar, Park Town, Chennai – 600003.
important_instructions: All candidates must ensure they fulfill all eligibility conditions before applying; admission to all stages of the examination is purely provisional and candidature can be rejected at any stage even after selection. One Time Registration (OTR) at www.tnpscexams.in is mandatory (OTR fee Rs. 150/-, valid for 5 years); Aadhaar linking with OTR is compulsory for all candidates. Candidates should apply online separately for each examination; OTR is not an application for any post. Candidates must choose subject papers only in which they have the prescribed educational qualification. Candidates shall be permitted to choose two district centres as their examination preference; only one centre for PwBD candidates. Request for change of examination centre will not be permitted. Hall Ticket (Memorandum of Admission) will be made available only on the Commission's website for download; it will not be sent by post. Appearance in both Paper I and Paper II is compulsory; non-appearance in either paper will result in non-consideration for selection. Paper II and Parts B & C of Paper I will be evaluated only if the candidate scores minimum 40% (60 marks) in Part A (Tamil Eligibility Test). Differently abled candidates can claim exemption from Part A (Tamil Eligibility Test) by furnishing required details in the online application; subsequent claims will not be entertained. Candidates must not bring mobile phones, electronic devices, smart watches, P&G Design Data Books, mathematical instruments, log tables, stencils, books, notes, or any other banned items into the examination hall. The Commission will publish results, onscreen certificate verification lists, and physical certificate verification dates only on www.tnpsc.gov.in; no individual communication will be sent by post. Candidates will be informed only through SMS and email. TNPSC recruitments are purely merit-based; candidates are cautioned against touts or agents making false promises of securing jobs through unfair means. In case of any doubt, the English version of the notification is final. Requests for change of exam date/time, relaxation of conditions, revaluation of answer sheets, or furnishing reasons for failure will not be entertained. During the process of recruitment, no information/clarification on selection particulars will be furnished under RTI Act until completion of the selection process.

faq.question: Q1. What is TNPSC CTS Examination (Non-Interview Posts) 2026?
faq.answer: TNPSC CTS Examination (Non-Interview Posts) 2026 is a recruitment drive by Tamil Nadu Public Service Commission under Notification No. 04/2026 to fill 461 vacancies across 45 technical posts in various Government Departments, PSUs, and Corporations of Tamil Nadu.

faq.question: Q2. How many vacancies are available in TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: A total of 461 vacancies are available across 45 posts including Assistant Engineer (64 posts for Agricultural Engineering, 50 for Rural Development, 38 for Highways), Block Health Statistician (62), Junior Photographer (23), Horticultural Officer (17), and others.

faq.question: Q3. What is the last date to apply for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: The last date for online application submission is 25 June 2026 (11:59 PM), with applications opening on 27 May 2026.

faq.question: Q4. What is the eligibility for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: Eligibility varies by post: candidates must have relevant Degree/PG Degree/Diploma (B.E./B.Tech for engineering posts, M.Sc. for scientific posts, MBA for managerial posts) from UGC/AICTE-recognized institutions with adequate knowledge in Tamil.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: Minimum age is 21 years as on 01 July 2026. Maximum age is 32 years for Others and 37 years for SC/ST/MBC/BC for most posts, with 10 years relaxation for PwBD candidates and varying limits for specific posts.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: The selection process includes a single-stage written examination with Paper I (OMR: Tamil Test 150 marks + General Studies 75 Qs + Aptitude 25 Qs = 150 marks) and Paper II (CBT: Subject Paper 200 Qs = 300 marks), followed by onscreen and physical certificate verification.

faq.question: Q7. Is there any application fee for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, examination fee is ₹100 per subject paper plus ₹150 One Time Registration fee for new registrants (valid for 5 years), payable online through Net Banking/Credit/Debit Card/UPI, with exemptions available for special categories.

faq.question: Q8. What is the exam pattern for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: Paper I consists of 100 questions (Tamil Eligibility Test) + 75 questions (General Studies) + 25 questions (Aptitude) in OMR mode for 3 hours. Paper II has 200 subject-specific questions in CBT mode for 3 hours, with total marks of 450.

faq.question: Q9. Is Tamil language knowledge mandatory for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: Yes, adequate Tamil knowledge is mandatory for most posts (must have studied Tamil at SSLC/HSC/Degree level or passed TNPSC Tamil Language Test). Candidates without proof must pass Second Class Language Test within 2 years of appointment or face discharge.

faq.question: Q10. How to apply for TNPSC CTS Non-Interview Posts 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must first complete One Time Registration (OTR) with Aadhaar linking at tnpscexams.in, then apply online from 27 May 2026 to 25 June 2026 by filling the application, uploading required documents, and paying the examination fee.
